versions.label: LSB
versions.text: 'Abide in Me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself unless it abides in the vine, so neither can you unless you abide in Me. I am the vine, you are the branches; he who abides in Me and I in him, he bears much fruit, for apart from Me you can do nothing.'

context_lines: In **John 15**, Jesus uses the vine and branches to teach abiding, fruitfulness, obedience, love, and dependence on Him. Requests in this chapter are framed by ongoing union with Christ, not by detached human wishing.
explanation_lines: This is the language the daily bread petition is actually speaking. It is the prayer form of abiding — a daily, ongoing cry for continued union with the only source of true life, not a request directed at circumstances or material need.
